decision_artifact_notice: This UI does not mutate GitHub. It only downloads an operator decision artifact bound to the pinned receipt and this context file.
recommended_use: Attach the downloaded JSON to a follow-up operator prompt.
Optionally commit it later under docs/receipts/operator-decisions/ as explicit operator evidence.
Use it as input to a separate queue-drain lane; do not treat the download itself as a merge, approval, label, ready, close, or request-changes action.

public_exposure.docs_site_possible: A redacted static copy can be exposed through docs.aragora.ai or aragora.ai after the packet/context is reviewed for private PR-comment content.
public_exposure.live_app_caution: The existing /review-queue backend is mutating; do not wire this worksheet into it unless a separate non-mutating settlement mode is built.

clusters.name: parser.py and CLI/docs merge-order cluster
clusters.summary: These PRs touch overlapping CLI parser/docs surfaces. Later PRs will need rebase after the first lands.
clusters.operator_guidance: Choose at most one first-settle candidate, then hold the rest until rebased. Draft members should normally stay held.
clusters.safe_default_action: Hold the cluster until the operator chooses first-settle order.
red_flags: Textual overlap in aragora/cli/parser.py and CLI/capability docs.
Tier-3 members require explicit human risk acceptance.
Some members are drafts or had in-flight checks at pin time.

choices.label: Settle #7240 first
choices.description: #7240 was ready and green at pin time but still needs explicit Tier-3 human risk acceptance.

choices.label: Settle #7215 first
choices.description: #7215 had one in-flight check at pin time and remains held as read-only metadata in this packet.

choices.label: Hold all parser/docs members
choices.description: Use when the operator wants a fresh head-pinned packet or a rebase plan before accepting any member.

clusters.name: worktree inventory duplicate-choice cluster
clusters.summary: #7256 and #7259 both change scripts/codex_worktree_value_inventory.py and should not both merge as-is.
clusters.operator_guidance: Pick one approach, or hold both and ask the author to fold the useful pieces into one PR.
clusters.safe_default_action: Hold both until the operator chooses one.
red_flags: Semantic duplicate: both modify the same worktree inventory behavior.
Textual overlap in scripts/codex_worktree_value_inventory.py and its tests.
#7256 had in-flight checks at pin time.

choices.label: Prefer #7256
choices.description: Choose the foreign worktree preservation approach; #7259 should close or rebase.

choices.label: Prefer #7259
choices.description: Choose the runtime-budget approach; #7256 should close or rebase.

choices.label: Hold both
choices.description: Use when the operator wants one reconciled PR instead of choosing between duplicates.
